name: Trail of the Windmills
name_original: Cerro de los Molinos — circular desde Alcublas
description: An easy 3.5-mile circular hike from Alcublas, featuring restored 17th-century windmills and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Alcublas offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, pine forests, and open areas with historical features. The region features varied terrain, including gentle slopes and more challenging ascents, often leading through natural ravines and offering expansive views. Trails provide opportunities to explore both natural beauty and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Alcublas The most popular hiking route is Trail of the Windmills, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This easy path features historical windmills and offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is GR 10: Montmayor Loop, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.5 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including forested sections and open hillsides. Local hikers also love the La Solana and Barranco Lucía Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through natural ravines and elevated sections, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Alcublas is defined by rolling hills, pine forests, and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alcublas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcublas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Alcublas?
A: Alcublas offers a good selection of hiking opportunities, with over 20 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more demanding ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Alcublas?
A: Yes, Alcublas has several easy trails. For a gentle and scenic walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317404384/trail-of-the-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Windmills&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.5-mile (5.7 km) path is relatively flat, features historical windmills, and offers panoramic views, making it ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hikes in Alcublas?
A: Hikes in Alcublas vary in length and duration. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317404384/trail-of-the-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Windmills&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 1 hour 40 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318109076/la-salada-peak-loop-via-gr-7-and-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salada Peak Loop via GR 7 and GR 10&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 8 hours to complete.

Q: Are there challenging hiking routes for experienced hikers in Alcublas?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318109076/la-salada-peak-loop-via-gr-7-and-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salada Peak Loop via GR 7 and GR 10&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.5-mile (23.3 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience through varied terrain.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Alcublas?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Alcublas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317964313/gr-10-montmayor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 10: Montmayor Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318034802/la-solana-and-barranco-lucia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Solana and Barranco Lucía Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the trails in Alcublas?
A: The trails around Alcublas offer a mix of natural beauty and points of interest. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2774487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda Spring&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/638741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montmayor Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4255052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya de La Espartosa&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. The region's landscape is characterized by rolling hills, pine forests, and natural ravines.

Q: Is Alcublas suitable for hiking with dogs?
A: Generally, the natural trails around Alcublas are suitable for hiking with dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alcublas?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Alcublas, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, which includes rolling hills, pine forests, and open areas, as well as the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any facilities or places to rest near the hiking routes in Alcublas?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail might be limited, the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2836821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcublas&lt;/a&gt; itself offers amenities. You might also find facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2774487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda Spring&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6525031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torrecillas Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, which can be good spots for a break.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcublas?
A: The best time for hiking in Alcublas is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while generally mild, can bring cooler temperatures.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Alcublas?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Alcublas. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as many routes start from accessible points within or just outside the village where street parking or small designated areas may be found.
tips:
Town located in the Valencian Community, in the Los Serranos region. Its economy is based on agriculture, especially crops such as vineyards, cereals, olive trees, almond trees and fig trees.

The most important monuments of the town are: the town hall from the 17th century and the church of San Antonio Abad from the same century.

Ascent to the promontory with the two adapted mills. There are three paths, one for hiking (I didn't travel it), one for bikes (quite technical, lots of loose stone and slopes on some sides of more than 20% (hike-a-bike friendly) and one for cars (the best option).

Picnic area, LOTS of wind, and great views.

I leave the description of the signage.


HISTORY
Alcublas, at a crossroads between the Turia and Palancia rivers and the Aragón road, is an agricultural municipality that between 1407 and 1835 was part of the estate of the Cartuja de Vall de Crist (Altura). Faced with the need to grind and after several lawsuits with the Cartuja, in 1644 the government of the town sent the parish priest Mossen Mañes and the master builder Pedro de Cubas to Xàbia to take a model for their mills In 1646 the blades and blades ran out machinery and on September 29 a French miller was contracted. The second mill was built later, modeled on the first and both were in operation until the 19th century.

THE MILL

In 2020, all the machinery was restored by Juan Bautista Sánchez, an expert miller.
The entire deck, the friar, of about 7t, can be moved to direct the blades towards the wind. dominant, displacing the steering stick with the donkey. The blades transmit the rotation. horizontal to the catalina wheel, which through the lantern is transformed into a vertical rotation that activates the flying stone. From the hopper, the grain passes through the flying stone and comes out through the gutter, turned into flour.
Beautiful views of the town of Alcublas, I recommend walking at the beginning of the year, when the almond trees are in bloom.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cerro de los Molinos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in the town of Alcublas. You can typically find parking within the town itself, close to the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de los Molinos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. In spring, you'll see blooming almond trees, while autumn provides comfortable weather and beautiful colors. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Cerro de los Molinos circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as easy and is well-suited for beginners and families. Its manageable distance of approximately 5.7 km and moderate elevation gain make it accessible for most fitness levels. There are some sections with a strong climb on a cobbled path and loose stones, but overall it's a rewarding experience without requiring advanced physical conditioning.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cerro de los Molinos trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, including easily accessible paths, sections with a strong climb on a cobbled path, and areas with loose stones. The route also winds through forested areas and almond groves, transitioning to dirt paths. It's generally well-maintained but sturdy footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the ascent to the summit of Alcublas Windmills, where you'll find two restored 17th-century windmills. From here, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views of the Sierra Calderona mountains, the village of Alcublas, and agricultural landscapes. You'll also pass by the 18th-century Ermita de Santa Lucía, a local historical landmark, and enjoy a View of Alcublas.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this circular route overlap with other official trails. You will find parts of the Senda de los Molinos, Camino de la Pedrosa, and the CIMA VA07 La Bellida along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts in the town of Alcublas, which offers basic services such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly small shops. At the summit near the Alcublas Windmills, there are picnic tables, making it an ideal spot for a break.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this easy hike, we recommend bringing comfortable walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and perhaps some snacks. Given the picnic tables at the windmills, you might consider packing a lunch to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach Alcublas?
faqs.answer: While Alcublas is an agricultural town, public transport options from larger cities like Valencia might be limited or infrequent. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance if you plan to rely on public transport.
